author: @mpirron1
text: Just wanted to comment that what you guys are doing is good. The reason why modern inventors actual products keep getting supressed is primarily their own fault, because they participate in the same activites they complain about. They hide how the invention works, put on unnecessary controls, file patents, trademarks, NDAs to hide behind IP laws. They start companies and/or sell out to someone that does the same thing or supresses the invention. You can't play In the mud and stay clean, not even a little, there is no such thing. Tesla's downfall too. Keep doing good.

author: @paulbrouyere1735
text: I like your video’s and the effort you put into them, but in this case you’re inducing a lot of unuseful drag using a washing mashine drum, with thousands of punched holes, in stead of a dryer drum, which has flat faces. Unless you want to make it go supersonic like an SR71, of course. I love your approach in fighting the elements though, so please keep trying new stuff out!

author: @jeffreywolsieffer4583
text:
3 Ways actually, and the strongest one - you've ignored and destroyed, "The Up-Draft Turbine."
. Updraft Turbines are used Everywhere including Chimney's ( to turn and rotate food ), Cooling Towers ( to Separate Heat from The Falling Cooled Waters ( Rain ) ), and Wind Generational Power-Towers which are usually a combination of One or usually All Designs - at once.
. . As for the Size blades, I take it you've never thought things through from the Vacuums point of view for Air Displacement - have you.!.??

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: What's the most common reason for washers to be thrown out? Leaking?

replies.author: @gravelydon7072
replies.text: Or the electronics goes out on them. Whirlpool, Samsung, Maytag, LG, and Fisher-Paykel in many models have direct drive motors that 3 phase magnet motors. Those are the ones that you really want as the motors in them can be readily turned into a DC source.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yeah, there's loads of problems that can happen, the last one I had. accidentally overloaded it, and it broke the concreate blocks inside LOL🤣🤣

replies.author: @salibaba
replies.text: I work in a store selling washing machines and we find a large amount of people replace hotpoint and hoover due to failing bearings, repairable yes, but cost prohibitive due to the labour pulling the entire machine apart in many cases.

replies.author: @gravelydon7072
replies.text: @salibaba Are most of those failed bearings caused by seals that failed or are the failed bearings the cause of the seal failures?

replies.author: @salibaba
replies.text: @gravelydon7072 don't know unfortunately. When it comes up most of the customers simply tell us the machine sounds like a jet plane taking off when spinning. It annoys me that such things can't be repaired cheaply but it's in the manufacturers best financial interest to have you buy a new one ,than spend more engineering them to be repaired easier.
